content.verdict: Meliá Llana Beach Resort & Spa is the highest-scoring honeymoon hotel on Sal and the cleanest adults-only 5-star option in Cape Verde — a 311-room all-inclusive on Santa Maria beach where the 16+ age limit does the heavy lifting and The Level premium service tier turns the experience into something closer to a boutique stay. For couples who want adults-only certainty, an all-inclusive that means it (no nickel-and-diming on dining, drinks, or premium spirits), and the rare combination of Africa-adjacent beach plus year-round 25°C+ climate, Meliá Llana is the most convincing answer in the destination. The Portuguese-Creole vibe — Cesária Évora's morna soundtrack drifts through Santa Maria town a short walk from the gates — gives the trip a cultural texture that distinguishes it from the homogenised Caribbean. The two pools, the spa, the beach club, and The Level lounge with its private check-in, dedicated concierge, and à la carte breakfast collectively make this the hotel that most consistently delivers the honeymoon brief on the island. It is what couples mean when they say they want "affordable Maldives" without pretending Cape Verde is the Maldives — different sea, different culture, different price tag, same quality of switching off.
content.best_room: The Adults-Only The Level Suite with Sea View is the best room on the property — a Level-tier suite with private check-in, à la carte Level breakfast, premium spirits in the minibar, the dedicated Level lounge for sundowners, and a sea-view terrace looking onto Santa Maria beach. For a smaller splurge, a standard The Level Room with Sea View delivers most of the same Level-tier perks at a softer price; honeymooners booking direct should always upgrade to The Level rather than staying in the standard wing.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & The Level Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Direct flight into Sal's Amílcar Cabral International Airport (SID), 15-minute transfer to Meliá Llana. The Level wing offers private check-in, a glass of sparkling, and a dedicated concierge to walk through the all-inclusive options. Slow afternoon at the Level pool, dinner at the resort's main buffet or one of the à la carte options included in the all-inclusive.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Adults-Only Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full day on Santa Maria beach from the Meliá Llana beach club, where the 16+ rule shows its value — the soundtrack stays calm, the loungers are spaced, and beach service runs without queue. The Atlantic is brilliantly clear but cool by tropical standards; the two warm pools are the bridge. Couples massage at the YHI spa in the late afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kitesurfing Lesson
itinerary_7_nights.description: Santa Maria is one of the world's premier kitesurfing destinations thanks to consistent trade winds and shallow flat water. A half-day intro lesson with a Santa Maria school is one of the great Cape Verde honeymoon stories. Lunch back at the Level pool grill, afternoon by the adults pool with a frozen cocktail.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pedra de Lume Salt Crater
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day excursion north to the Pedra de Lume volcanic crater, where a hypersaline lake inside an extinct volcano lets you float effortlessly. Pink-tinged salt pans, ochre crater walls — Sal's most photogenic landscape. Lunch in Espargos at a catchupa restaurant, return to Meliá Llana for the best sundown of the week from the Level lounge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Desert Quad Biking
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day quad-biking circuit across Sal's volcanic moonscape — abandoned salt mines, the Buracona blue eye sea cave on the wild west coast, deserted northern beaches. Back at the resort by mid-afternoon for the spa thermal circuit, then dinner at the Italian or seafood à la carte restaurant — both included in the all-inclusive.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran Sunset Sail
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late-afternoon catamaran sail from Santa Maria pier with snorkelling stops over the reef and frequent dolphin sightings. Sundown return into Santa Maria as the resort lights come on. Quiet dinner back at Meliá Llana's gourmet restaurant — the most romantic of the on-site venues.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cesária Évora Music Night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final beach morning, long lunch at the Level pool, then walk into Santa Maria town for the last evening — dinner at one of the catch-of-the-day seafood places followed by live morna and coladeira at a Cesária Évora music bar. The Portuguese-Creole sound is the cultural soul of Cape Verde and the right note to end on.

caveats: December–February is the Harmattan dust season — Saharan dust can leave skies hazy and the trade winds peak; perfect for kitesurfers, less ideal for sunbathing.
The Atlantic water is cool by tropical standards (around 22–24°C year-round); the warm pools are the fallback for couples who like bathwater swimming.
Sal's east coast has documented shark presence; only swim from the monitored Santa Maria south-coast beach in front of the resort.
The 16+ adults-only rule is enforced on-property but cannot guarantee a child-free experience on the public beach itself; the difference inside the gates is still significant.

faqs.question: Is Meliá Llana truly adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — the entire resort is restricted to guests aged 16 and over. The 16+ threshold (rather than 18+) is the only nuance worth noting; in practice the atmosphere is firmly adult, calm, and couples-skewed, with around 78% of TripAdvisor reviews coming from couples.

faqs.question: What does The Level service tier add?
faqs.answer: The Level is Meliá's premium room category — private check-in, dedicated concierge, à la carte Level breakfast, premium spirits in the minibar, access to the exclusive Level lounge for sundowners and snacks, and Level-only pool and beach areas. For honeymooners, upgrading to The Level is the single best decision at this hotel.

faqs.question: Sal or Boavista — which is better for a Cape Verde honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Sal is the right pick for first-time Cape Verde honeymooners — more 5-star choice, better restaurants in Santa Maria, the strongest kitesurfing, and Meliá Llana itself, which has no Boavista equivalent at this quality. Boavista is wilder and quieter but with fewer adults-only options.

faqs.question: Is the all-inclusive worth it?
faqs.answer: Yes, decisively — the all-inclusive at Meliá Llana includes all à la carte restaurants, premium spirits, room service, and most beach activities. Cape Verde's outside-resort dining is limited compared to Caribbean destinations, so the AI plan is the right default; couples wanting variety can still walk into Santa Maria town for one or two evenings.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March through June and October through November are the sweet spots — warm, sunny, manageable winds. December–February is peak season with the strongest winds and occasional Saharan dust. July–September is hot and humid with rare short showers.

faqs.question: How long is the flight to Cape Verde?
faqs.answer: About 6 hours direct from London, 4 hours from Lisbon, 6 hours from Amsterdam or Frankfurt, and roughly the same time zone as the UK in winter — jet lag is essentially zero, which is a real advantage for a short honeymoon.
